'''Daniel Holdsworth''' (born 27 April 1984<ref name="foxsportspulse1"/>) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] [[Rugby league positions#Stand Off / Five-eighth|five-eighth]] who played in the 2000s and 2010s.

==Background==
Born in the northern [[New South Wales]] town, [[Tweed Heads]], Holdsworth moved to [[Perth]] in [[Western Australia]] at an early age. In Perth, Holdsworth played all his junior rugby league for the [[North Beach Sea Eagles]] in the [[Western Australia Rugby League|Perth Rugby League]] competition.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.theleader.com.au/story/2124900/dj-looks-set-for-chance-in-halves/|title=DJ looks set for chance in halves|author=Forrest, Brad|newspaper=St George & Sutherland Shire Leader|date=6 March 2014|accessdate=15 May 2015}}</ref> Holdsworth represented Western Australia at the Under 15 and Under 18 age groups.<ref name="foxsportspulse1"/>

==Career==
Holdsworth spent two years in the [[Penrith Panthers]]' lower grades,<ref name="foxsportspulse1"/> before signing with the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] for the 2004 season. He made his NRL début while at the club and played 6 games in two seasons, before joining Canterbury, where he would play 70 games between 2006 and 2009.

Holdsworth then spent four seasons in the [[Super League]], playing for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. He represented the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] in 2012 and was described by [[England national rugby league team|English]] fullback Sam Tomkins as "very clever, powerful and an effective stand off".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://menmedia.co.uk/manchestereveningnews/sport/rugby_league/s/1586467_sam-tomkins-column-we-must-be-ruthless-against-salford-reds |title=Sam Tomkins column: We must be ruthless against Salford Reds |work= Manchester Evening News |access-date=6 September 2012 |archive-date=21 April 2013 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130421000337/http://menmedia.co.uk/manchestereveningnews/sport/rugby_league/s/1586467_sam-tomkins-column-we-must-be-ruthless-against-salford-reds |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In 2014, Holdsworth returned to the NRL, signing with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref name="sharks1"/>
He played 8 games for Cronulla in his only season at the club as they finished last on the table and claimed the wooden spoon.  This was mainly attributed to the club's high injury toll and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/24/sharks-set-for-wooden-spoon-after-defeat-to-raiders/|title=Sharks set for wooden spoon after defeat to Raiders|website=The Guardian|location=London|date=24 August 2014}}</ref>

==Personal==
In January 2014, Holdsworth's [[Caringbah, New South Wales|Caringbah]] home was robbed and memorabilia stolen, including signed jerseys from former teammates [[Sonny Bill Williams]] and [[Andrew Ryan (rugby league)|Andrew Ryan]].<ref>[http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/cronulla-sharks-recruit-daniel-holdsworth-has-house-robbed-20140127-31j6a.html Cronulla Sharks recruit Daniel Holdsworth has house robbed], AAP, dated 27 January 2014.</ref>
